   295→def generate_pipeline_report(
   296→    candidates: List[CandidateBasic],
   297→    results: dict,
   298→    keywords: Tuple[str, ...],
   299→    template: str,
   300→    mode: SearchMode,
   301→) -> str:
   302→    """Generate an Excel report for a pipeline run.
   303→
   304→    Args:
   305→        candidates: All candidates from search
   306→        results: Send results dict (total/sent/failed/skipped/rate_limited/details)
   307→        keywords: Search keywords used
   308→        template: Template name used
   309→        mode: Search mode (search_center or enterprise)
   310→
   311→    Returns:
   312→        Path to the generated report file
   313→    """
   314→    from openpyxl import Workbook
   315→    from openpyxl.styles import Font, PatternFill, Alignment, Border, Side
   316→    from loguru import logger
   317→
   318→    timestamp = datetime.now()
   319→    config = get_config()
   320→    report_dir = Path(config.get("data.report_dir", "data/reports"))
   321→    report_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   322→    report_path = report_dir / f"pipeline_{timestamp.strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')}.xlsx"
   323→
   324→    wb = Workbook()
   325→
   326→    # --- Sheet 1: Overview ---
   327→    ws_overview = wb.active
   328→    ws_overview.title = "概览"
   329→
   330→    header_font = Font(bold=True, color="FFFFFF")
   331→    header_fill = PatternFill(start_color="4472C4", end_color="4472C4", fill_type="solid")
   332→    border = Border(
   333→        left=Side(style="thin"),
   334→        right=Side(style="thin"),
   335→        top=Side(style="thin"),
   336→        bottom=Side(style="thin"),
   337→    )
   338→
   339→    overview_rows = [
   340→        ("执行时间", timestamp.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")),
   341→        ("搜索关键词", ", ".join(keywords)),
   342→        ("消息模板", template),
   343→        ("搜索模式", mode.value),
   344→        ("搜索结果数", results["total"]),
   345→        ("发送总数", results["sent"] + results["failed"]),
   346→        ("成功", results["sent"]),
   347→        ("失败", results["failed"]),
   348→        ("跳过(已沟通)", results["skipped"]),
   349→        ("限流", results["rate_limited"]),
   350→    ]
   351→
   352→    for r_idx, (key, value) in enumerate(overview_rows, 1):
   353→        cell_key = ws_overview.cell(row=r_idx, column=1, value=key)
   354→        cell_val = ws_overview.cell(row=r_idx, column=2, value=value)
   355→        cell_key.font = Font(bold=True)
   356→        for cell in (cell_key, cell_val):
   357→            cell.border = border
   358→            cell.alignment = Alignment(vertical="center")
   359→
   360→    ws_overview.column_dimensions["A"].width = 18
   361→    ws_overview.column_dimensions["B"].width = 40
   362→
   363→    # --- Sheet 2: Candidate Details ---
   364→    ws_details = wb.create_sheet(title="候选人详情")
   365→
   366→    columns = ["序号", "姓名", "职位", "公司", "地区", "脉脉ID", "沟通状态", "发送内容", "失败原因"]
   367→    for c_idx, col_name in enumerate(columns, 1):
   368→        cell = ws_details.cell(row=1, column=c_idx, value=col_name)
   369→        cell.font = header_font
   370→        cell.fill = header_fill
   371→        cell.alignment = Alignment(horizontal="center", vertical="center")
   372→        cell.border = border
   373→
   374→    # Build a lookup from maimai_id -> detail
   375→    details_by_id: Dict[str, dict] = {}
   376→    for detail in results.get("details", []):
   377→        details_by_id[detail["maimai_id"]] = detail
   378→
   379→    status_map = {
   380→        "sent": "已发送",
   381→        "failed": "失败",
   382→        "skipped": "跳过",
   383→        "rate_limited": "限流",
   384→    }
   385→
   386→    for r_idx, c in enumerate(candidates, 1):
   387→        detail = details_by_id.get(c.maimai_id, {})
   388→        raw_status = detail.get("status", "not_attempted")
   389→        status_label = status_map.get(raw_status, "未处理")
   390→        reason = detail.get("reason", "")
   391→
   392→        content = detail.get("content", "")
   393→        row_data = [
   394→            r_idx,
   395→            c.name,
   396→            c.title,
   397→            c.company,
   398→            c.location or "",
   399→            c.maimai_id,
   400→            status_label,
   401→            content,
   402→            reason,
   403→        ]
   404→        for c_idx, value in enumerate(row_data, 1):
   405→            cell = ws_details.cell(row=r_idx + 1, column=c_idx, value=value)
   406→            cell.border = border
   407→            cell.alignment = Alignment(vertical="center", wrap_text=True)
   408→
   409→    detail_widths = {"A": 6, "B": 12, "C": 25, "D": 20, "E": 12, "F": 18, "G": 12, "H": 40, "I": 30}
   410→    for col, width in detail_widths.items():
   411→        ws_details.column_dimensions[col].width = width
   412→
   413→    ws_details.freeze_panes = "A2"
   414→    ws_details.auto_filter.ref = ws_details.dimensions
   415→
   416→    wb.save(str(report_path))
   417→    logger.info(f"Pipeline report saved to: {report_path}")
   418→    console.print(f"[green]Pipeline report saved to: {report_path}[/green]")
   419→    return str(report_path)

   421→
   422→def save_run_history(
   423→    keywords: Tuple[str, ...],
   424→    template: str,
   425→    mode: SearchMode,
   426→    results: dict,
   427→    dry_run: bool,
   428→    report_path: Optional[str] = None,
   429→) -> None:
   430→    """Append a pipeline run record to JSON and regenerate Markdown summary.
   431→
   432→    Produces two files under data/:
   433→    - run_history.json: machine-readable array of all runs
   434→    - run_history.md: human-readable Markdown table
   435→    """
   436→    import json
   437→    from loguru import logger
   438→
   439→    data_dir = Path("data")
   440→    data_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   441→    json_path = data_dir / "run_history.json"
   442→    md_path = data_dir / "run_history.md"
   443→
   444→    # Read existing records
   445→    records: List[dict] = []
   446→    if json_path.exists():
   447→        try:
   448→            records = json.loads(json_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   449→        except (json.JSONDecodeError, IOError):
   450→            logger.warning("Failed to read run_history.json, starting fresh")
   451→
   452→    # Build new record
   453→    timestamp = datetime.now()
   454→    record = {
   455→        "timestamp": timestamp.isoformat(timespec="seconds"),
   456→        "keywords": list(keywords),
   457→        "template": template,
   458→        "mode": mode.value if isinstance(mode, SearchMode) else mode,
   459→        "dry_run": dry_run,
   460→        "total": results.get("total", 0),
   461→        "sent": results.get("sent", 0),
   462→        "failed": results.get("failed", 0),
   463→        "skipped": results.get("skipped", 0),
   464→        "rate_limited": results.get("rate_limited", 0),
   465→        "report_file": Path(report_path).name if report_path else None,
   466→    }
   467→    records.append(record)
   468→
   469→    # Write JSON
   470→    json_path.write_text(json.dumps(records, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
   471→
   472→    # Regenerate Markdown from full history
   473→    lines = [
   474→        "# Pipeline 运行历史",
   475→        "",
   476→        "| 时间 | 关键词 | 模板 | 模式 | 搜索数 | 发送 | 失败 | 跳过 | 限流 | 报告 |",
   477→        "|------|--------|------|------|--------|------|------|------|------|------|",
   478→    ]
   479→    for r in records:
   480→        ts = r["timestamp"][:16].replace("T", " ")
   481→        kws = ", ".join(r["keywords"])
   482→        tpl = r["template"]
   483→        m = r["mode"]
   484→        total = str(r["total"])
   485→        if r.get("dry_run"):
   486→            sent = failed = skipped = rate_limited = "-"
   487→        else:
   488→            sent = str(r["sent"])
   489→            failed = str(r["failed"])
   490→            skipped = str(r["skipped"])
   491→            rate_limited = str(r["rate_limited"])
   492→        report = r.get("report_file") or "-"
   493→        lines.append(
   494→            f"| {ts} | {kws} | {tpl} | {m} | {total} | {sent} | {failed} | {skipped} | {rate_limited} | {report} |"
   495→        )
   496→
   497→    md_path.write_text("\n".join(lines) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
   498→    logger.info(f"Run history updated: {json_path}")
   499→    console.print(f"[dim]运行历史已更新: {md_path}[/dim]")
   500→
   501→
   502→def display_candidates_table(candidates: List[CandidateBasic], title: str) -> None:
   503→    """Display a Rich table of candidates."""
   504→    table = Table(title=f"{title} ({len(candidates)} candidates)")
   505→    table.add_column("#", style="dim", justify="right")
   506→    table.add_column("Name", style="cyan")
   507→    table.add_column("Title", style="green")
   508→    table.add_column("Company", style="yellow")
   509→    table.add_column("Location", style="blue")
   510→    has_scores = any(getattr(c, "score", None) is not None for c in candidates[:1])
   511→    if has_scores:
   512→        table.add_column("Score", style="magenta", justify="right")
   513→    table.add_column("ID", style="dim")
   514→
   515→    for i, c in enumerate(candidates[:50], 1):
   516→        row = [str(i), c.name, c.title, c.company, c.location or ""]
   517→        if has_scores:
   518→            row.append(str(getattr(c, "score", "")))
   519→        row.append(c.maimai_id)
   520→        table.add_row(*row)
   521→
   522→    console.print(table)
   523→
   524→    if len(candidates) > 50:
   525→        console.print(f"[dim]... and {len(candidates) - 50} more[/dim]")
   526→

   528→def display_send_results(
   529→    results: dict, title: str = "Send Results", quota: Optional[dict] = None
   530→) -> None:
   531→    """Display send results summary table."""
   532→    console.print()
   533→    result_table = Table(title=title)
   534→    result_table.add_column("Metric", style="bold")
   535→    result_table.add_column("Count", justify="right")
   536→
   537→    result_table.add_row("Total candidates", str(results["total"]))
   538→    result_table.add_row("Sent", f"[green]{results['sent']}[/green]")
   539→    result_table.add_row("Failed", f"[red]{results['failed']}[/red]")
   540→    result_table.add_row("Skipped (already messaged)", f"[yellow]{results['skipped']}[/yellow]")
   541→    result_table.add_row("Rate limited", f"[yellow]{results['rate_limited']}[/yellow]")
   542→    if quota:
   543→        result_table.add_row("联系券剩余", f"[cyan]{quota.get('uh_left', '?')}[/cyan]")
   544→
   545→    console.print(result_table)

   548→async def run_pipeline_default(
   549→    browser: BrowserManager,
   550→    keywords: Tuple[str, ...],
   551→    company: Tuple[str, ...],
   552→    location: Optional[str],
   553→    max_pages: int,
   554→    template: str,
   555→    variables: Dict[str, str],
   556→    dry_run: bool,
   557→    skip_messaged: bool,
   558→    interactive: bool = False,
   559→    mode: SearchMode = SearchMode.SEARCH_CENTER,
   560→    skip_confirmation: bool = False,
   561→    exchange_phone: bool = False,
   562→    exchange_wechat: bool = False,
   563→    min_years: Optional[int] = None,
   564→) -> None:
   565→    """Pipeline: search all pages first, then send messages.
   566→
   567→    Saves a checkpoint after search completes so that message sending
   568→    can be resumed if interrupted.
   569→    """
   570→    checkpoint = CheckpointManager()
   571→
   572→    # Talent bank: search + send per keyword (page changes between keywords)
   573→    if mode == SearchMode.TALENT_BANK and not dry_run:
   574→        all_candidates: List[CandidateBasic] = []
   575→        seen_ids: Set[str] = set()
   576→        combined_results = {
   577→            "total": 0,
   578→            "sent": 0,
   579→            "failed": 0,
   580→            "skipped": 0,
   581→            "rate_limited": 0,
   582→            "details": [],
   583→        }
   584→
   585→        for idx, kw in enumerate(keywords):
   586→            console.print(f"\n[bold blue][{idx+1}/{len(keywords)}] Talent bank: {kw}[/bold blue]")
   587→            kw_candidates = await search_candidates_talent_bank(
   588→                keywords=kw,
   589→                company=company,
   590→                pages=max_pages,
   591→                browser=browser,
   592→                min_years=min_years if idx == 0 else None,
   593→            )
   594→            # Deduplicate
   595→            new_candidates = []
   596→            for c in kw_candidates:
   597→                if c.maimai_id not in seen_ids:
   598→                    seen_ids.add(c.maimai_id)
   599→                    new_candidates.append(c)
   600→            all_candidates.extend(new_candidates)
   601→            console.print(f"  Found {len(new_candidates)} new candidates (deduplicated)")
   602→
   603→            if not new_candidates:
   604→                continue
   605→
   606→            display_candidates_table(new_candidates, f"Keyword: {kw}")
   607→
   608→            # Send messages immediately while page shows these results
   609→            kw_results = await send_batch(
   610→                new_candidates,
   611→                template,
   612→                variables,
   613→                [kw],
   614→                browser,
   615→                skip_messaged,
   616→                skip_confirmation=skip_confirmation,
   617→                mode=SearchMode.TALENT_BANK,
   618→                exchange_phone=exchange_phone,
   619→                exchange_wechat=exchange_wechat,
   620→            )
   621→            for key in ("total", "sent", "failed", "skipped", "rate_limited"):
   622→                combined_results[key] += kw_results.get(key, 0)
   623→            combined_results["details"].extend(kw_results.get("details", []))
   624→
   625→        if not all_candidates:
   626→            console.print("[yellow]No candidates found for any keyword.[/yellow]")
   627→            return
   628→
   629→        quota = await fetch_contact_quota(browser)
   630→        display_send_results(combined_results, quota=quota)
   631→        report_path = generate_pipeline_report(
   632→            all_candidates, combined_results, keywords, template, mode
   633→        )
   634→        save_run_history(
   635→            keywords, template, mode, combined_results, dry_run=False, report_path=report_path
   636→        )
   637→        checkpoint.delete()
   638→        console.print("[dim]Checkpoint cleared (pipeline complete)[/dim]")
   639→        _auto_backup_database()
   640→        return
   641→
   642→    if mode == SearchMode.ENTERPRISE:
   643→        # Keep enterprise tab open for inline messaging (unless dry_run)
   644→        all_candidates, seen_ids = await search_all_keywords_enterprise(
   645→            browser,
   646→            keywords,
   647→            company,
   648→            max_pages,
   649→            keep_last_tab_open=not dry_run,
   650→        )
   651→    elif mode == SearchMode.TALENT_BANK:
   652→        all_candidates, seen_ids = await search_all_keywords_talent_bank(
   653→            browser,
   654→            keywords,
   655→            company,
   656→            max_pages,
   657→            min_years=min_years,
   658→        )
   659→    else:
   660→        all_candidates, seen_ids = await search_all_keywords(
   661→            browser,
   662→            keywords,
   663→            company,
   664→            location,
   665→            max_pages,
   666→        )
   667→
   668→    if not all_candidates:
   669→        console.print("[yellow]No candidates found for any keyword.[/yellow]")
   670→        return
   671→
   672→    # Save checkpoint after search (batch_pages=0 marks non-batch mode)
   673→    _save_checkpoint(
   674→        checkpoint,
   675→        keywords,
   676→        template,
   677→        variables,
   678→        company,
   679→        location,
   680→        max_pages,
   681→        0,
   682→        skip_messaged,
   683→        interactive,
   684→        max_pages,
   685→        [],
   686→        seen_ids,
   687→        {"total": 0, "sent": 0, "failed": 0, "skipped": 0, "rate_limited": 0},
   688→        mode=mode.value,
   689→        dry_run=dry_run,
   690→        exchange_phone=exchange_phone,
   691→        exchange_wechat=exchange_wechat,
   692→        min_years=min_years,
   693→    )
   694→
   695→    display_candidates_table(all_candidates, "Pipeline Results")
   696→
   697→    if dry_run:
   698→        console.print(
   699→            f"\n[cyan][Dry Run] {len(all_candidates)} candidates found. No messages sent.[/cyan]"
   700→        )
   701→        save_run_history(
   702→            keywords,
   703→            template,
   704→            mode,
   705→            {"total": len(all_candidates), "sent": 0, "failed": 0, "skipped": 0, "rate_limited": 0},
   706→            dry_run=True,
   707→        )
   708→        checkpoint.delete()
   709→        return
   710→
   711→    results = await send_batch(
   712→        all_candidates,
   713→        template,
   714→        variables,
   715→        list(keywords),
   716→        browser,
   717→        skip_messaged,
   718→        interactive,
   719→        skip_confirmation=skip_confirmation,
   720→        mode=mode,
   721→    )
   722→
   723→    # Fetch final quota for summary display
   724→    quota = await fetch_contact_quota(browser)
   725→    display_send_results(results, quota=quota)
   726→    report_path = generate_pipeline_report(all_candidates, results, keywords, template, mode)
   727→    save_run_history(keywords, template, mode, results, dry_run=False, report_path=report_path)
   728→
   729→    # Clean up checkpoint on successful completion
   730→    checkpoint.delete()
   731→    console.print("[dim]Checkpoint cleared (pipeline complete)[/dim]")
   732→
   733→    # Auto backup database after successful pipeline
   734→    _auto_backup_database()
   735→
   736→
   737→async def run_pipeline_batched(
   738→    browser: BrowserManager,
   739→    keywords: Tuple[str, ...],
   740→    company: Tuple[str, ...],
   741→    location: Optional[str],
   742→    max_pages: int,
   743→    batch_pages: int,
   744→    template: str,
   745→    variables: Dict[str, str],
   746→    dry_run: bool,
   747→    skip_messaged: bool,
   748→    interactive: bool = False,
   749→    resume_ckpt=None,
   750→    mode: SearchMode = SearchMode.SEARCH_CENTER,
   751→    skip_confirmation: bool = False,
   752→    exchange_phone: bool = False,
   753→    exchange_wechat: bool = False,
   754→    min_years: Optional[int] = None,
   755→) -> None:
   756→    """Batched pipeline: search N pages, send messages, repeat."""
   757→    # Restore state from checkpoint or start fresh
   758→    if resume_ckpt:
   759→        # On resume, only treat sent_ids as truly "seen" (skip-worthy).
   760→        # Candidates that were found but not sent should be retried.
   761→        sent_ids = list(resume_ckpt.sent_ids)
   762→        seen_ids = set(sent_ids)  # only skip candidates already successfully sent
   763→        total_results = resume_ckpt.total_results or {
   764→            "total": 0,
   765→            "sent": 0,
   766→            "failed": 0,
   767→            "skipped": 0,
   768→            "rate_limited": 0,
   769→        }
   770→        start_from_page = resume_ckpt.completed_pages + 1
   771→        console.print(
   772→            f"[cyan]Resuming from page {start_from_page} (sent: {len(sent_ids)}, retrying unsent)[/cyan]"
   773→        )
   774→        checkpoint = resume_ckpt
   775→    else:
   776→        seen_ids = set()
   777→        sent_ids = []
   778→        total_results = {"total": 0, "sent": 0, "failed": 0, "skipped": 0, "rate_limited": 0}
   779→        start_from_page = 1
   780→        checkpoint = CheckpointManager()
   781→
   782→    all_candidates: List[CandidateBasic] = []
   783→    all_details: List[dict] = []
   784→    batch_num = 0
   785→    consecutive_empty_batches = 0
   786→
   787→    batch_start = start_from_page
   788→    while batch_start <= max_pages:
   789→        batch_page_count = min(batch_pages, max_pages - batch_start + 1)
   790→        batch_num += 1
   791→        batch_end = batch_start + batch_page_count - 1
   792→
   793→        console.print(
   794→            f"\n[bold magenta]━━━ Batch {batch_num}: Pages {batch_start}-{batch_end} ━━━[/bold magenta]"
   795→        )
   796→
   797→        if mode == SearchMode.ENTERPRISE:
   798→            batch_candidates, _ = await search_all_keywords_enterprise(
   799→                browser,
   800→                keywords,
   801→                company,
   802→                batch_page_count,
   803→                keep_last_tab_open=not dry_run,
   804→            )
   805→        elif mode == SearchMode.TALENT_BANK:
   806→            batch_candidates, _ = await search_all_keywords_talent_bank(
   807→                browser,
   808→                keywords,
   809→                company,
   810→                batch_page_count,
   811→                min_years=min_years if batch_num == 1 else None,
   812→            )
   813→        else:
   814→            batch_candidates, _ = await search_all_keywords(
   815→                browser,
   816→                keywords,
   817→                company,
   818→                location,
   819→                batch_page_count,
   820→                start_page=batch_start,
   821→            )
   822→        # Filter out globally seen IDs
   823→        batch_candidates = [c for c in batch_candidates if c.maimai_id not in seen_ids]
   824→        seen_ids.update(c.maimai_id for c in batch_candidates)
   825→        all_candidates.extend(batch_candidates)
   826→
   827→        if not batch_candidates:
   828→            consecutive_empty_batches += 1
   829→            console.print(f"[yellow]No new candidates in batch {batch_num}.[/yellow]")
   830→
   831→            if consecutive_empty_batches >= 2:
   832→                console.print(
   833→                    "[red]Multiple consecutive empty batches detected. Verifying login status...[/red]"
   834→                )
   835→                if not await check_login_status(browser.page):
   836→                    console.print(
   837→                        "[red]Session expired! Clearing session and re-authenticating...[/red]"
   838→                    )
   839→                    session_manager = get_session_manager()
   840→                    await session_manager.clear()
   841→                    await ensure_logged_in(browser)
   842→                    consecutive_empty_batches = 0
   843→                    console.print("[green]Re-authenticated. Retrying current batch...[/green]")
   844→                    batch_num -= 1
   845→                    continue
   846→                else:
   847→                    console.print(
   848→                        "[yellow]Session appears valid. Possibly no more results for these keywords.[/yellow]"
   849→                    )
   850→
   851→            _save_checkpoint(
   852→                checkpoint,
   853→                keywords,
   854→                template,
   855→                variables,
   856→                company,
   857→                location,
   858→                max_pages,
   859→                batch_pages,
   860→                skip_messaged,
   861→                interactive,
   862→                batch_end,
   863→                sent_ids,
   864→                seen_ids,
   865→                total_results,
   866→                mode=mode.value,
   867→                dry_run=dry_run,
   868→                exchange_phone=exchange_phone,
   869→                exchange_wechat=exchange_wechat,
   870→                min_years=min_years,
   871→            )
   872→            batch_start += batch_pages
   873→            continue
   874→        else:
   875→            consecutive_empty_batches = 0
   876→
   877→        display_candidates_table(batch_candidates, f"Batch {batch_num} Results")
   878→
   879→        if dry_run:
   880→            console.print(
   881→                f"\n[cyan][Dry Run] Batch {batch_num}: {len(batch_candidates)} candidates found. No messages sent.[/cyan]"
   882→            )
   883→            _save_checkpoint(
   884→                checkpoint,
   885→                keywords,
   886→                template,
   887→                variables,
   888→                company,
   889→                location,
   890→                max_pages,
   891→                batch_pages,
   892→                skip_messaged,
   893→                interactive,
   894→                batch_end,
   895→                sent_ids,
   896→                seen_ids,
   897→                total_results,
   898→                mode=mode.value,
   899→                dry_run=dry_run,
   900→                exchange_phone=exchange_phone,
   901→                exchange_wechat=exchange_wechat,
   902→                min_years=min_years,
   903→            )
   904→            batch_start += batch_pages
   905→            continue
   906→
   907→        results = await send_batch(
   908→            batch_candidates,
   909→            template,
   910→            variables,
   911→            list(keywords),
   912→            browser,
   913→            skip_messaged,
   914→            interactive,
   915→            skip_confirmation=skip_confirmation,
   916→            mode=mode,
   917→            exchange_phone=exchange_phone,
   918→            exchange_wechat=exchange_wechat,
   919→        )
   920→        batch_quota = await fetch_contact_quota(browser)
   921→        display_send_results(results, title=f"Batch {batch_num} Send Results", quota=batch_quota)
   922→
   923→        all_details.extend(results.get("details", []))
   924→        for detail in results.get("details", []):
   925→            if detail.get("status") == "sent":
   926→                sent_ids.append(detail["maimai_id"])
   927→
   928→        for key in total_results:
   929→            total_results[key] += results[key]
   930→
   931→        _save_checkpoint(
   932→            checkpoint,
   933→            keywords,
   934→            template,
   935→            variables,
   936→            company,
   937→            location,
   938→            max_pages,
   939→            batch_pages,
   940→            skip_messaged,
   941→            interactive,
   942→            batch_end,
   943→            sent_ids,
   944→            seen_ids,
   945→            total_results,
   946→            mode=mode.value,
   947→            dry_run=dry_run,
   948→            exchange_phone=exchange_phone,
   949→            exchange_wechat=exchange_wechat,
   950→            min_years=min_years,
   951→        )
   952→        console.print(f"[dim]Checkpoint saved (page {batch_end})[/dim]")
   953→
   954→        batch_start += batch_pages
   955→
   956→    # Final summary
   957→    console.print(f"\n[bold magenta]━━━ Pipeline Complete ━━━[/bold magenta]")
   958→    console.print(f"Total unique candidates found: {len(seen_ids)}")
   959→    report_path = None
   960→    if not dry_run and total_results["total"] > 0:
   961→        final_quota = await fetch_contact_quota(browser)
   962→        display_send_results(total_results, title="Overall Send Results", quota=final_quota)
   963→        total_results_with_details = {**total_results, "details": all_details}
   964→        report_path = generate_pipeline_report(
   965→            all_candidates, total_results_with_details, keywords, template, mode
   966→        )
   967→
   968→    history_results = (
   969→        total_results
   970→        if not dry_run
   971→        else {
   972→            "total": len(seen_ids),
   973→            "sent": 0,
   974→            "failed": 0,
   975→            "skipped": 0,
   976→            "rate_limited": 0,
   977→        }
   978→    )
   979→    save_run_history(keywords, template, mode, history_results, dry_run, report_path)
   980→
   981→    checkpoint.delete()
   982→    console.print("[dim]Checkpoint cleared (pipeline complete)[/dim]")
   983→
   984→    # Auto backup database after successful batched pipeline
   985→    _auto_backup_database()
   986→
   987→
   988→def _save_checkpoint(
   989→    checkpoint,
   990→    keywords,
   991→    template,
   992→    variables,
   993→    company,
   994→    location,
   995→    max_pages,
   996→    batch_pages,
   997→    skip_messaged,
   998→    interactive,
   999→    completed_pages,
  1000→    sent_ids,
  1001→    seen_ids,
  1002→    total_results,
  1003→    mode: str = "search_center",
  1004→    dry_run: bool = False,
  1005→    exchange_phone: bool = False,
  1006→    exchange_wechat: bool = False,
  1007→    min_years: int = None,
  1008→) -> None:
  1009→    """Save pipeline checkpoint (DRY helper)."""
  1010→    checkpoint.save(
  1011→        keywords=keywords,
  1012→        template=template,
  1013→        variables=variables,
  1014→        company=company,
  1015→        location=location,
  1016→        max_pages=max_pages,
  1017→        batch_pages=batch_pages,
  1018→        skip_messaged=skip_messaged,
  1019→        interactive=interactive,
  1020→        completed_pages=completed_pages,
  1021→        sent_ids=sent_ids,
  1022→        seen_ids=list(seen_ids),
  1023→        total_results=total_results,
  1024→        mode=mode,
  1025→        dry_run=dry_run,
  1026→        exchange_phone=exchange_phone,
  1027→        exchange_wechat=exchange_wechat,
  1028→        min_years=min_years,
  1029→    )
  1030→

1→"""Anti-detection module for browser automation.
     2→
     3→Implements human-like interaction via Emunium + Patchright:
     4→- Human-like scrolling behavior (chunked, variable speed)
     5→- Human-like clicking via Emunium (natural mouse movement)
     6→- Human-like typing via Emunium (variable keystroke timing)
     7→
     8→Note: Webdriver property hiding and navigator masking are handled
     9→by Patchright automatically in CDP mode.
    10→"""
    11→
    12→import random
    13→
    14→from loguru import logger
    15→from patchright.async_api import Page
    16→from emunium import EmuniumPlaywright
    17→
    18→from src.exceptions.errors import SelectorNotFoundError
    19→
    20→
    21→async def human_scroll(page: Page, direction: str = 'down', distance: int = None) -> None:
    22→    """
    23→    Perform human-like scrolling with variable speed.
    24→
    25→    Args:
    26→        page: Playwright page instance
    27→        direction: 'up' or 'down'
    28→        distance: Scroll distance in pixels (random if None)
    29→    """
    30→    if direction not in ('up', 'down'):
    31→        raise ValueError(f"Invalid scroll direction: {direction!r}, expected 'up' or 'down'")
    32→
    33→    if distance is None:
    34→        distance = random.randint(200, 600)
    35→
    36→    if direction == 'up':
    37→        distance = -distance
    38→
    39→    # Break scroll into smaller chunks
    40→    chunks = random.randint(3, 8)
    41→    chunk_size = distance // chunks
    42→
    43→    for i in range(chunks):
    44→        # Add variance to each chunk
    45→        actual_chunk = chunk_size + random.randint(-20, 20)
    46→
    47→        await page.mouse.wheel(0, actual_chunk)
    48→
    49→        # Variable delay between scroll chunks
    50→        delay = random.uniform(0.05, 0.15)
    51→        await page.wait_for_timeout(delay * 1000)
    52→
    53→    # Small pause after scrolling (human would look at content)
    54→    await page.wait_for_timeout(random.uniform(0.2, 0.5) * 1000)
    55→
    56→
    57→async def human_click(page: Page, selector: str) -> None:
    58→    """
    59→    Click an element with human-like behavior using Emunium.
    60→
    61→    Emunium provides natural mouse movement and click simulation.
    62→
    63→    Args:
    64→        page: Playwright page instance
    65→        selector: CSS selector for element to click
    66→    """
    67→    try:
    68→        element = await page.wait_for_selector(selector, timeout=10000)
    69→    except Exception as e:
    70→        if "timeout" in str(type(e).__name__).lower() or "timeout" in str(e).lower():
    71→            raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element not found: {selector}")
    72→        raise
    73→
    74→    if element is None:
    75→        raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element resolved to None: {selector}")
    76→
    77→    try:
    78→        emunium = EmuniumPlaywright(page)
    79→        await emunium.click_at(element)
    80→    except Exception:
    81→        # Fallback: emunium may fail in CDP mode (viewport_size=None → TypeError,
    82→        # or other internal errors). Native click is reliable.
    83→        logger.debug("Emunium click failed, falling back to native click")
    84→        await element.click()
    85→
    86→
    87→async def human_type(page: Page, selector: str, text: str) -> None:
    88→    """
    89→    Type text with human-like timing using Emunium.
    90→
    91→    Args:
    92→        page: Playwright page instance
    93→        selector: CSS selector for input element
    94→        text: Text to type
    95→    """
    96→    try:
    97→        element = await page.wait_for_selector(selector, timeout=10000)
    98→    except Exception as e:
    99→        if "timeout" in str(type(e).__name__).lower() or "timeout" in str(e).lower():
   100→            raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element not found: {selector}")
   101→        raise
   102→
   103→    if element is None:
   104→        raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element resolved to None: {selector}")
   105→
   106→    try:
   107→        emunium = EmuniumPlaywright(page)
   108→        await emunium.type_at(element, text)
   109→    except Exception:
   110→        # Fallback: emunium may fail in CDP mode (viewport_size=None → TypeError,
   111→        # or other internal errors). Native fill+type is reliable.
   112→        logger.debug("Emunium type failed, falling back to native type")
   113→        await element.fill("")
   114→        await page.keyboard.type(text, delay=random.randint(50, 150))
   115→
   116→
   117→

1→"""Browser manager for Playwright automation.
     2→
     3→Provides a singleton browser manager that connects to an existing Chrome
     4→instance via CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol). Supports multi-tab management
     5→for workflows that open new tabs (e.g., enterprise recruitment).
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import asyncio
     9→from pathlib import Path
    10→from typing import List, Optional
    11→from datetime import datetime
    12→
    13→from patchright.async_api import async_playwright, Browser, BrowserContext, Page, Playwright
    14→from loguru import logger
    15→
    16→from src.utils.config import get_config, Config
    17→from src.core.session import SessionManager, get_session_manager
    18→from src.exceptions.errors import BrowserError
    19→
    20→
    21→class BrowserManager:
    22→    """
    23→    Singleton browser manager for Maimai automation.
    24→
    25→    Connects to a user-managed Chrome instance via CDP endpoint.
    26→    The user starts Chrome with --remote-debugging-port=9222 and logs in
    27→    manually. This manager connects/disconnects without closing Chrome.
    28→    """
    29→
    30→    _instance: Optional['BrowserManager'] = None
    31→    _lock: Optional[asyncio.Lock] = None
    32→    _lock_loop: Optional[asyncio.AbstractEventLoop] = None  # track which loop owns _lock
    33→
    34→    def __new__(cls, **kwargs):
    35→        if cls._instance is None:
    36→            cls._instance = super().__new__(cls)
    37→            cls._instance._initialized = False
    38→        return cls._instance
    39→
    40→    def __init__(
    41→        self,
    42→        config: Optional[Config] = None,
    43→        session_manager: Optional[SessionManager] = None,
    44→    ):
    45→        if self._initialized:
    46→            return
    47→
    48→        self._playwright: Optional[Playwright] = None
    49→        self._browser: Optional[Browser] = None
    50→        self._context: Optional[BrowserContext] = None
    51→        self._page: Optional[Page] = None
    52→        self._primary_page: Optional[Page] = None  # Original tab to return to
    53→        self._opened_tabs: List[Page] = []  # Tabs opened during our session
    54→        self._session_manager: SessionManager = session_manager or get_session_manager()
    55→        self._config: Config = config or get_config()
    56→        self._cdp_endpoint: Optional[str] = None
    57→        self._owned_page: bool = False  # True if we created the page (vs reusing existing)
    58→        self._initialized = True
    59→
    60→    @property
    61→    def is_running(self) -> bool:
    62→        """Check if browser is currently connected."""
    63→        return self._browser is not None and self._browser.is_connected()
    64→
    65→    @property
    66→    def is_connected(self) -> bool:
    67→        """Read-only connection status check for API endpoints."""
    68→        return self.is_running
    69→
    70→    @property
    71→    def page(self) -> Optional[Page]:
    72→        """Get current page instance."""
    73→        return self._page
    74→
    75→    @property
    76→    def context(self) -> Optional[BrowserContext]:
    77→        """Get current browser context."""
    78→        return self._context
    79→
    80→    @classmethod
    81→    def _ensure_lock(cls):
    82→        """Lazily create the asyncio.Lock inside a running event loop.
    83→
    84→        Recreates the lock if the event loop has changed (e.g. between
    85→        separate asyncio.run() calls) to avoid 'attached to a different
    86→        event loop' RuntimeError.
    87→        """
    88→        current_loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
    89→        if cls._lock is None or cls._lock_loop is not current_loop:
    90→            cls._lock = asyncio.Lock()
    91→            cls._lock_loop = current_loop
    92→
    93→    def set_cdp_endpoint(self, endpoint: str) -> None:
    94→        """Override CDP endpoint (e.g. from CLI --cdp flag)."""
    95→        self._cdp_endpoint = endpoint
    96→
    97→    async def start(self) -> Page:
    98→        """
    99→        Connect to Chrome via CDP and return a page.
   100→
   101→        Returns:
   102→            Configured Playwright page instance
   103→
   104→        Raises:
   105→            BrowserError: If connection fails
   106→        """
   107→        self._ensure_lock()
   108→        async with self._lock:
   109→            if self.is_running:
   110→                logger.debug("Browser already connected, returning existing page")
   111→                return self._page
   112→
   113→            try:
   114→                # Determine CDP endpoint
   115→                endpoint = self._cdp_endpoint or self._config.get(
   116→                    'browser.cdp_endpoint', 'http://localhost:9222'
   117→                )
   118→                timeout = self._config.get('browser.timeout', 30000)
   119→
   120→                logger.info(f"Connecting to Chrome via CDP: {endpoint}")
   121→
   122→                # Start Playwright
   123→                self._playwright = await async_playwright().start()
   124→
   125→                # Connect to existing Chrome via CDP
   126→                self._browser = await self._playwright.chromium.connect_over_cdp(endpoint)
   127→
   128→                # Get the default context (the user's browser context with cookies)
   129→                contexts = self._browser.contexts
   130→                if contexts:
   131→                    self._context = contexts[0]
   132→                else:
   133→                    # Fallback: create a new context (shouldn't happen with CDP)
   134→                    logger.warning("No existing context found, creating new one")
   135→                    self._context = await self._browser.new_context()
   136→
   137→                # Set default timeout
   138→                self._context.set_default_timeout(timeout)
   139→
   140→                # Reuse existing page or create one
   141→                pages = self._context.pages
   142→                if pages:
   143→                    self._page = pages[0]
   144→                    self._owned_page = False
   145→                    logger.info(f"Reusing existing tab: {self._page.url}")
   146→                else:
   147→                    self._page = await self._context.new_page()
   148→                    self._owned_page = True
   149→                    logger.info("Created new tab")
   150→
   151→                # Remember the primary tab
   152→                self._primary_page = self._page
   153→                self._opened_tabs = []
   154→
   155→                logger.info("Connected to Chrome via CDP successfully")
   156→                return self._page
   157→
   158→            except Exception as e:
   159→                logger.error(f"Failed to connect to Chrome via CDP: {e}")
   160→                await self._close_internal()
   161→                raise BrowserError(
   162→                    f"Failed to connect to Chrome via CDP at {endpoint}. "
   163→                    f"Make sure Chrome is running with --remote-debugging-port=9222. "
   164→                    f"Error: {e}"
   165→                )
   166→
   167→    async def close(self) -> None:
   168→        """Disconnect from Chrome via CDP. Does NOT close the user's Chrome."""
   169→        self._ensure_lock()
   170→        async with self._lock:
   171→            await self._close_internal()
   172→
   173→    async def _close_internal(self) -> None:
   174→        """Actual cleanup logic — callable without acquiring _lock.
   175→
   176→        Must only be called while the lock is already held, or from
   177→        start()'s except block (which already holds the lock).
   178→        """
   179→        try:
   180→            # Close tabs we opened during the session
   181→            for tab in self._opened_tabs:
   182→                try:
   183→                    if not tab.is_closed():
   184→                        await tab.close()
   185→                except Exception:
   186→                    pass  # Tab may already be closed by user
   187→            self._opened_tabs = []
   188→
   189→            # Only close pages we created, not the user's existing tabs
   190→            if self._page and self._owned_page:
   191→                try:
   192→                    await self._page.close()
   193→                except Exception:
   194→                    pass  # Page may already be closed
   195→            self._page = None
   196→            self._primary_page = None
   197→            self._owned_page = False
   198→
   199→            # Don't close the context — it belongs to the user's Chrome
   200→            self._context = None
   201→
   202→            # browser.close() in CDP mode just disconnects, doesn't kill Chrome
   203→            if self._browser:
   204→                try:
   205→                    await self._browser.close()
   206→                except Exception as e:
   207→                    logger.error(f"Error closing browser connection: {e}")
   208→                finally:
   209→                    self._browser = None
   210→
   211→            if self._playwright:
   212→                try:
   213→                    await self._playwright.stop()
   214→                except Exception as e:
   215→                    logger.error(f"Error stopping Playwright: {e}")
   216→                finally:
   217→                    self._playwright = None
   218→
   219→            logger.info("Disconnected from Chrome (browser stays open)")
   220→
   221→        except Exception as e:
   222→            logger.error(f"Error disconnecting from Chrome: {e}")
   223→
   224→    async def wait_for_new_tab(self, timeout: int = 10000) -> Page:
   225→        """Wait for a click-triggered new tab, apply stealth, and switch to it.
   226→
   227→        Call this right after performing an action that opens a new tab
   228→        (e.g., clicking a link with target=_blank).
   229→
   230→        Args:
   231→            timeout: Max wait time in milliseconds
   232→
   233→        Returns:
   234→            The new Page instance (also set as the active page)
   235→
   236→        Raises:
   237→            BrowserError: If no new tab appears within timeout
   238→        """
   239→        if not self._context:
   240→            raise BrowserError("No active context for wait_for_new_tab")
   241→
   242→        try:
   243→            new_page = await self._context.wait_for_event(
   244→                "page", timeout=timeout
   245→            )
   246→            await new_page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded")
   247→            self._opened_tabs.append(new_page)
   248→            self._page = new_page
   249→            logger.info(f"New tab captured: {new_page.url}")
   250→            return new_page
   251→        except Exception as e:
   252→            raise BrowserError(f"No new tab opened within {timeout}ms: {e}")
   253→
   254→    def switch_to_primary_tab(self) -> Page:
   255→        """Switch back to the original (primary) tab.
   256→
   257→        Returns:
   258→            The primary Page instance
   259→        """
   260→        if not self._primary_page or self._primary_page.is_closed():
   261→            raise BrowserError("Primary tab is not available")
   262→
   263→        self._page = self._primary_page
   264→        logger.debug(f"Switched to primary tab: {self._page.url}")
   265→        return self._page
   266→
   267→    async def close_current_tab(self) -> Page:
   268→        """Close the current active tab and switch to the previous one.
   269→
   270→        Will not close the primary tab. After closing, switches to the
   271→        most recent remaining tab or falls back to the primary tab.
   272→
   273→        Returns:
   274→            The page that became active after closing
   275→
   276→        Raises:
   277→            BrowserError: If trying to close the primary tab
   278→        """
   279→        if not self._page:
   280→            raise BrowserError("No active page to close")
   281→
   282→        if self._page == self._primary_page:
   283→            raise BrowserError("Cannot close the primary tab")
   284→
   285→        closing = self._page
   286→        try:
   287→            # Remove from opened_tabs tracking
   288→            if closing in self._opened_tabs:
   289→                self._opened_tabs.remove(closing)
   290→            await closing.close()
   291→        except Exception as e:
   292→            logger.warning(f"Error closing tab: {e}")
   293→
   294→        # Switch to previous tab
   295→        if self._context and self._context.pages:
   296→            self._page = self._context.pages[-1]
   297→        else:
   298→            self._page = self._primary_page
   299→
   300→        logger.debug(f"Closed tab, switched to: {self._page.url if self._page else 'none'}")
   301→        return self._page
   302→
   303→    async def save_session(self) -> None:
   304→        """Save current session to disk (backup of cookies)."""
   305→        if self._context:
   306→            await self._session_manager.save(self._context)
   307→        else:
   308→            logger.warning("No active context to save session from")
   309→
   310→    async def screenshot(self, name: str = None) -> str:
   311→        """
   312→        Take a screenshot of current page.
   313→
   314→        Args:
   315→            name: Screenshot name (timestamp used if None)
   316→
   317→        Returns:
   318→            Path to saved screenshot
   319→        """
   320→        if not self._page:
   321→            raise BrowserError("No active page for screenshot")
   322→
   323→        screenshot_dir = Path(self._config.get('data.screenshot_dir', 'data/screenshots'))
   324→        screenshot_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   325→
   326→        if name is None:
   327→            name = datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')
   328→
   329→        filepath = screenshot_dir / f"{name}.png"
   330→
   331→        await self._page.screenshot(path=str(filepath), full_page=True)
   332→        logger.info(f"Screenshot saved: {filepath}")
   333→
   334→        return str(filepath)
   335→
   336→
   337→
   338→# Global browser manager instance
   339→_browser_manager: Optional[BrowserManager] = None
   340→
   341→
   342→def get_browser_manager() -> BrowserManager:
   343→    """Get global browser manager instance."""
   344→    global _browser_manager
   345→    if _browser_manager is None:
   346→        _browser_manager = BrowserManager()
   347→    return _browser_manager
   348→

1→"""Rate limiter for preventing account bans."""
     2→
     3→import asyncio
     4→from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
     5→from typing import Tuple, Optional
     6→
     7→from loguru import logger
     8→from sqlalchemy import func
     9→
    10→from src.models.database import ActionLog, get_db_manager, DatabaseManager
    11→from src.utils.config import get_config, Config
    12→from src.exceptions.errors import RateLimitExceededError
    13→
    14→
    15→class RateLimiter:
    16→    """Rate limiter for controlling action frequency.
    17→
    18→    Supports adaptive throttling: when captchas or errors are reported,
    19→    limits are temporarily tightened to reduce detection risk.
    20→    """
    21→
    22→    def __init__(self, config: Optional[Config] = None, db_manager: Optional[DatabaseManager] = None):
    23→        """Initialize rate limiter with configuration.
    24→
    25→        Args:
    26→            config: Config instance (uses global singleton if None)
    27→            db_manager: DatabaseManager instance (uses global singleton if None)
    28→        """
    29→        self.config = config or get_config()
    30→        self.db_manager = db_manager or get_db_manager()
    31→
    32→        # Adaptive throttle state
    33→        self._throttle_factor: float = 1.0  # 1.0 = normal, <1.0 = stricter
    34→        self._captcha_timestamps: list = []
    35→        self._pruned: bool = False  # one-time prune flag
    36→
    37→    def report_captcha(self) -> None:
    38→        """Report a captcha occurrence to trigger adaptive throttling."""
    39→        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    40→        self._captcha_timestamps.append(now)
    41→        # Keep only last 30 minutes of captcha events
    42→        cutoff = now - timedelta(minutes=30)
    43→        self._captcha_timestamps = [t for t in self._captcha_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    44→        self._update_throttle_factor()
    45→
    46→    def _update_throttle_factor(self) -> None:
    47→        """Recalculate throttle factor based on recent captcha frequency.
    48→
    49→        Throttle factor ranges from 1.0 (normal) to 0.3 (heavily throttled).
    50→        - 1 captcha in 30min: reduce to 0.7x (30% fewer operations allowed)
    51→        - 2 captchas: reduce to 0.5x
    52→        - 3+ captchas: reduce to 0.3x
    53→        """
    54→        captcha_count = len(self._captcha_timestamps)
    55→
    56→        if captcha_count >= 3:
    57→            self._throttle_factor = 0.3
    58→        elif captcha_count >= 2:
    59→            self._throttle_factor = 0.5
    60→        elif captcha_count >= 1:
    61→            self._throttle_factor = 0.7
    62→        else:
    63→            self._throttle_factor = 1.0
    64→
    65→        if self._throttle_factor < 1.0:
    66→            logger.warning(
    67→                f"Adaptive throttle active: factor={self._throttle_factor:.1f} "
    68→                f"(captchas={captcha_count} in last 30min)"
    69→            )
    70→
    71→    def _refresh_throttle(self) -> None:
    72→        """Prune expired timestamps and recalculate throttle factor."""
    73→        cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=30)
    74→        self._captcha_timestamps = [t for t in self._captcha_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    75→        self._update_throttle_factor()
    76→
    77→    @property
    78→    def throttle_factor(self) -> float:
    79→        """Current adaptive throttle factor (1.0 = normal, <1.0 = throttled)."""
    80→        return self._throttle_factor
    81→
    82→    def _get_limits(self, action_type: str) -> Tuple[int, int, int]:
    83→        """
    84→        Get rate limits for an action type, adjusted by adaptive throttle.
    85→
    86→        Args:
    87→            action_type: Type of action ('search', 'view_profile', 'send_message')
    88→
    89→        Returns:
    90→            Tuple of (per_hour_limit, per_day_limit, min_interval_seconds)
    91→        """
    92→        action_map = {
    93→            'search': ('search_per_hour', 'search_per_day'),
    94→            'view_profile': ('profile_view_per_hour', 'profile_view_per_day'),
    95→            'send_message': ('message_per_hour', 'message_per_day'),
    96→        }
    97→
    98→        if action_type not in action_map:
    99→            raise ValueError(f"Unknown action type: {action_type}")
   100→
   101→        hour_key, day_key = action_map[action_type]
   102→
   103→        per_hour = self.config.get(f'rate_limits.{hour_key}', 20)
   104→        per_day = self.config.get(f'rate_limits.{day_key}', 100)
   105→        min_interval = self.config.get('rate_limits.min_interval_seconds', 30)
   106→
   107→        # Apply adaptive throttle: reduce limits and increase interval
   108→        per_hour = max(1, int(per_hour * self._throttle_factor))
   109→        per_day = max(1, int(per_day * self._throttle_factor))
   110→        if self._throttle_factor < 1.0:
   111→            min_interval = int(min_interval / self._throttle_factor)
   112→
   113→        return per_hour, per_day, min_interval
   114→
   115→    def _count_actions(self, action_type: str, since: datetime) -> int:
   116→        """
   117→        Count actions of a type since a given time.
   118→
   119→        Args:
   120→            action_type: Type of action
   121→            since: Count actions since this time
   122→
   123→        Returns:
   124→            Number of actions
   125→        """
   126→        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   127→            count = session.query(func.count(ActionLog.id)).filter(
   128→                ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   129→                ActionLog.timestamp >= since
   130→            ).scalar()
   131→
   132→            return count or 0
   133→
   134→    @staticmethod
   135→    def _ensure_aware(dt: Optional[datetime]) -> Optional[datetime]:
   136→        """Attach UTC timezone to naive datetimes returned by SQLite."""
   137→        if dt is not None and dt.tzinfo is None:
   138→            return dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
   139→        return dt
   140→
   141→    def _get_last_action_time(self, action_type: Optional[str] = None) -> Optional[datetime]:
   142→        """
   143→        Get timestamp of last action.
   144→
   145→        Args:
   146→            action_type: Type of action, or None for any action
   147→
   148→        Returns:
   149→            Timestamp of last action (timezone-aware), or None if no actions
   150→        """
   151→        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   152→            query = session.query(func.max(ActionLog.timestamp))
   153→
   154→            if action_type:
   155→                query = query.filter(ActionLog.action_type == action_type)
   156→
   157→            last_time = query.scalar()
   158→            return self._ensure_aware(last_time)
   159→
   160→    def check_rate_limit(self, action_type: str) -> None:
   161→        """
   162→        Check if action is allowed under rate limits.
   163→
   164→        Args:
   165→            action_type: Type of action to check
   166→
   167→        Raises:
   168→            RateLimitExceededError: If rate limit is exceeded
   169→        """
   170→        # Prune stale entries once per session to keep counts accurate
   171→        if not self._pruned:
   172→            self.prune_old_entries()
   173→            self._pruned = True
   174→
   175→        self._refresh_throttle()
   176→        per_hour, per_day, min_interval = self._get_limits(action_type)
   177→
   178→        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   179→        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
   180→        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)
   181→
   182→        # Check hourly limit
   183→        hourly_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
   184→        if hourly_count >= per_hour:
   185→            # Calculate wait time until oldest action expires
   186→            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   187→                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
   188→                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   189→                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_hour_ago
   190→                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()
   191→
   192→                if oldest_in_window:
   193→                    oldest_ts = self._ensure_aware(oldest_in_window[0])
   194→                    wait_until = oldest_ts + timedelta(hours=1)
   195→                    wait_seconds = max(1, int((wait_until - now).total_seconds()))
   196→                else:
   197→                    wait_seconds = 3600
   198→
   199→            raise RateLimitExceededError(
   200→                action_type=action_type,
   201→                wait_time=wait_seconds,
   202→                message=f"Hourly rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
   203→                        f"Limit: {per_hour}/hour. Wait {wait_seconds // 60} minutes."
   204→            )
   205→
   206→        # Check daily limit
   207→        daily_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
   208→        if daily_count >= per_day:
   209→            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   210→                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
   211→                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   212→                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_day_ago
   213→                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()
   214→
   215→                if oldest_in_window:
   216→                    oldest_ts = self._ensure_aware(oldest_in_window[0])
   217→                    wait_until = oldest_ts + timedelta(days=1)
   218→                    wait_seconds = max(1, int((wait_until - now).total_seconds()))
   219→                else:
   220→                    wait_seconds = 86400
   221→
   222→            raise RateLimitExceededError(
   223→                action_type=action_type,
   224→                wait_time=wait_seconds,
   225→                message=f"Daily rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
   226→                        f"Limit: {per_day}/day. Wait {wait_seconds // 3600} hours."
   227→            )
   228→
   229→        # Check minimum interval (per-action-type to avoid cross-type blocking)
   230→        last_action = self._get_last_action_time(action_type)
   231→        if last_action:
   232→            elapsed = (now - last_action).total_seconds()
   233→            if elapsed < min_interval:
   234→                wait_seconds = max(1, int(min_interval - elapsed))
   235→                raise RateLimitExceededError(
   236→                    action_type=action_type,
   237→                    wait_time=wait_seconds,
   238→                    message=f"Minimum interval not met for {action_type}. Wait {wait_seconds} seconds."
   239→                )
   240→
   241→        logger.debug(f"Rate limit check passed for {action_type}")
   242→
   243→    def log_action(self, action_type: str, details: Optional[dict] = None) -> None:
   244→        """
   245→        Log an action to the database.
   246→
   247→        Args:
   248→            action_type: Type of action
   249→            details: Optional action details
   250→        """
   251→        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   252→            action_log = ActionLog(
   253→                action_type=action_type,
   254→                timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
   255→                details=details or {}
   256→            )
   257→            session.add(action_log)
   258→
   259→        logger.debug(f"Logged action: {action_type}")
   260→
   261→    def prune_old_entries(self, retention_hours: int = 48) -> int:
   262→        """Remove ActionLog entries older than the retention window.
   263→
   264→        This prevents stale counts from accumulating across sessions and
   265→        keeps the ActionLog table from growing indefinitely.
   266→
   267→        Args:
   268→            retention_hours: Delete entries older than this many hours (default 48)
   269→
   270→        Returns:
   271→            Number of entries deleted
   272→        """
   273→        cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=retention_hours)
   274→        try:
   275→            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   276→                count = session.query(ActionLog).filter(
   277→                    ActionLog.timestamp < cutoff
   278→                ).delete()
   279→                logger.debug(f"Pruned {count} old ActionLog entries (older than {retention_hours}h)")
   280→                return count
   281→        except Exception as e:
   282→            logger.error(f"Failed to prune ActionLog entries: {e}")
   283→            return 0
   284→
   285→    def get_quota_status(self, action_type: str) -> dict:
   286→        """
   287→        Get current quota status for an action type.
   288→
   289→        Args:
   290→            action_type: Type of action
   291→
   292→        Returns:
   293→            Dictionary with quota information
   294→        """
   295→        per_hour, per_day, _ = self._get_limits(action_type)
   296→
   297→        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   298→        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
   299→        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)
   300→
   301→        hourly_used = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
   302→        daily_used = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
   303→
   304→        return {
   305→            'action_type': action_type,
   306→            'hourly': {
   307→                'used': hourly_used,
   308→                'limit': per_hour,
   309→                'remaining': max(0, per_hour - hourly_used)
   310→            },
   311→            'daily': {
   312→                'used': daily_used,
   313→                'limit': per_day,
   314→                'remaining': max(0, per_day - daily_used)
   315→            }
   316→        }
   317→
   318→
   319→async def async_check_rate_limit(
   320→    action_type: str,
   321→    max_retries: int = 60,
   322→    max_total_wait: int = 1800,
   323→) -> None:
   324→    """
   325→    Check rate limit, waiting through any violations until the action is allowed.
   326→
   327→    Blocks until the rate limit clears. Raises RateLimitExceededError if
   328→    still blocked after max_retries attempts or max_total_wait seconds
   329→    (prevents hanging for hours when daily limits are hit).
   330→
   331→    Args:
   332→        action_type: Type of action to check
   333→        max_retries: Maximum wait-retry cycles before giving up
   334→        max_total_wait: Maximum total seconds to wait before giving up (default 30 min)
   335→    """
   336→    import time
   337→    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
   338→    start = time.monotonic()
   339→    for attempt in range(max_retries):
   340→        try:
   341→            rate_limiter.check_rate_limit(action_type)
   342→            return
   343→        except RateLimitExceededError as e:
   344→            elapsed = time.monotonic() - start
   345→            if attempt >= max_retries - 1 or elapsed + e.wait_time > max_total_wait:
   346→                raise
   347→            wait = e.wait_time + 1  # +1s buffer
   348→            logger.info(f"Rate limit hit: {e}. Waiting {wait}s before retrying (attempt {attempt + 1}/{max_retries}, elapsed {elapsed:.0f}s)...")
   349→            await asyncio.sleep(wait)
   350→
   351→
   352→# Global rate limiter instance
   353→_rate_limiter = None
   354→
   355→
   356→def get_rate_limiter() -> RateLimiter:
   357→    """Get global rate limiter instance."""
   358→    global _rate_limiter
   359→    if _rate_limiter is None:
   360→        _rate_limiter = RateLimiter()
   361→    return _rate_limiter
   362→

1→"""Pipeline checkpoint manager for resume capability.
     2→
     3→Saves pipeline progress to a JSON file so that interrupted pipelines
     4→can be resumed from where they left off.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→import json
     8→import os
     9→import tempfile
    10→from datetime import datetime, timezone
    11→from pathlib import Path
    12→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Set
    13→
    14→from loguru import logger
    15→
    16→
    17→def _get_checkpoint_dir() -> Path:
    18→    """Resolve checkpoint directory from config or use default."""
    19→    try:
    20→        from src.utils.config import get_config
    21→
    22→        config = get_config()
    23→        return Path(config.get("data.checkpoint_dir", "data/.checkpoints"))
    24→    except Exception:
    25→        return Path("data/.checkpoints")
    26→
    27→
    28→class CheckpointManager:
    29→    """Manages pipeline checkpoint save/load for resume support."""
    30→
    31→    def __init__(self, checkpoint_id: Optional[str] = None):
    32→        checkpoint_dir = _get_checkpoint_dir()
    33→        checkpoint_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    34→
    35→        if checkpoint_id:
    36→            self.checkpoint_path = checkpoint_dir / f"{checkpoint_id}.json"
    37→        else:
    38→            # Generate ID from timestamp
    39→            ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    40→            self.checkpoint_path = checkpoint_dir / f"pipeline_{ts}.json"
    41→
    42→        self._data: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    43→
    44→    def save(
    45→        self,
    46→        keywords: tuple,
    47→        template: str,
    48→        variables: dict,
    49→        company: tuple,
    50→        location: Optional[str],
    51→        max_pages: int,
    52→        batch_pages: int,
    53→        skip_messaged: bool,
    54→        interactive: bool,
    55→        completed_pages: int,
    56→        sent_ids: List[str],
    57→        seen_ids: List[str],
    58→        total_results: Optional[dict] = None,
    59→        mode: str = "search_center",
    60→        dry_run: bool = False,
    61→        exchange_phone: bool = False,
    62→        exchange_wechat: bool = False,
    63→        min_years: Optional[int] = None,
    64→    ) -> None:
    65→        """Save current pipeline state to checkpoint file."""
    66→        self._data = {
    67→            "created_at": self._data.get("created_at", datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()),
    68→            "updated_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
    69→            "params": {
    70→                "keywords": list(keywords),
    71→                "template": template,
    72→                "variables": variables,
    73→                "company": list(company) if company else [],
    74→                "location": location,
    75→                "max_pages": max_pages,
    76→                "batch_pages": batch_pages,
    77→                "skip_messaged": skip_messaged,
    78→                "interactive": interactive,
    79→                "mode": mode,
    80→                "dry_run": dry_run,
    81→                "exchange_phone": exchange_phone,
    82→                "exchange_wechat": exchange_wechat,
    83→                "min_years": min_years,
    84→            },
    85→            "progress": {
    86→                "completed_pages": completed_pages,
    87→                "sent_ids": sent_ids,
    88→                "seen_ids": seen_ids,
    89→                "total_results": total_results or {},
    90→            },
    91→        }
    92→
    93→        try:
    94→            tmp_fd, tmp_path = tempfile.mkstemp(dir=self.checkpoint_path.parent, suffix=".tmp")
    95→            try:
    96→                with os.fdopen(tmp_fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    97→                    json.dump(self._data, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
    98→                os.replace(tmp_path, self.checkpoint_path)
    99→            except BaseException:
   100→                try:
   101→                    os.unlink(tmp_path)
   102→                except OSError:
   103→                    pass
   104→                raise
   105→            logger.debug(f"Checkpoint saved: {self.checkpoint_path}")
   106→        except Exception as e:
   107→            logger.error(f"Failed to save checkpoint: {e}")
   108→            raise
   109→
   110→    @classmethod
   111→    def load_latest(cls) -> Optional["CheckpointManager"]:
   112→        """Load the most recent checkpoint file.
   113→
   114→        Returns:
   115→            CheckpointManager with loaded data, or None if no checkpoint exists.
   116→        """
   117→        checkpoint_dir = _get_checkpoint_dir()
   118→        checkpoint_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   119→        files = sorted(
   120→            checkpoint_dir.glob("pipeline_*.json"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True
   121→        )
   122→
   123→        if not files:
   124→            return None
   125→
   126→        mgr = cls(checkpoint_id="__loading__")
   127→        mgr.checkpoint_path = files[0]
   128→
   129→        try:
   130→            with open(files[0], "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   131→                mgr._data = json.load(f)
   132→            logger.info(f"Loaded checkpoint: {files[0]}")
   133→            return mgr
   134→        except Exception as e:
   135→            logger.error(f"Failed to load checkpoint: {e}")
   136→            return None
   137→
   138→    @property
   139→    def params(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   140→        return self._data.get("params", {})
   141→
   142→    @property
   143→    def progress(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   144→        return self._data.get("progress", {})
   145→
   146→    @property
   147→    def completed_pages(self) -> int:
   148→        return self.progress.get("completed_pages", 0)
   149→
   150→    @property
   151→    def sent_ids(self) -> List[str]:
   152→        return list(self.progress.get("sent_ids", []))
   153→
   154→    @property
   155→    def seen_ids(self) -> Set[str]:
   156→        return set(self.progress.get("seen_ids", []))
   157→
   158→    @property
   159→    def total_results(self) -> Dict[str, int]:
   160→        return self.progress.get("total_results", {})
   161→
   162→    @property
   163→    def summary(self) -> str:
   164→        """Human-readable summary of checkpoint state."""
   165→        p = self.params
   166→        pr = self.progress
   167→        created = self._data.get("created_at", "unknown")
   168→        updated = self._data.get("updated_at", "unknown")
   169→        mode = p.get("mode", "search_center")
   170→        return (
   171→            f"Keywords: {p.get('keywords', [])}\n"
   172→            f"Template: {p.get('template', '')}\n"
   173→            f"Mode: {mode}\n"
   174→            f"Pages completed: {pr.get('completed_pages', 0)} / {p.get('max_pages', '?')}\n"
   175→            f"Candidates seen: {len(pr.get('seen_ids', []))}\n"
   176→            f"Messages sent: {len(pr.get('sent_ids', []))}\n"
   177→            f"Created: {created}\n"
   178→            f"Last updated: {updated}"
   179→        )
   180→
   181→    def delete(self) -> None:
   182→        """Remove checkpoint file after successful completion."""
   183→        try:
   184→            if self.checkpoint_path.exists():
   185→                self.checkpoint_path.unlink()
   186→                logger.debug(f"Checkpoint deleted: {self.checkpoint_path}")
   187→        except Exception as e:
   188→            logger.error(f"Failed to delete checkpoint: {e}")
   189→

1→"""Database models and setup using SQLAlchemy."""
     2→
     3→from contextlib import contextmanager
     4→from datetime import datetime, timezone
     5→from pathlib import Path
     6→from typing import Optional
     7→
     8→from sqlalchemy import (
     9→    Column, Integer, String, Text, DateTime, JSON,
    10→    ForeignKey, create_engine, Index, CheckConstraint, event
    11→)
    12→from sqlalchemy.orm import declarative_base, sessionmaker, relationship
    13→from sqlalchemy.pool import QueuePool, StaticPool
    14→from loguru import logger
    15→
    16→from src.utils.config import get_config
    17→
    18→Base = declarative_base()
    19→
    20→
    21→def _utcnow():
    22→    """Return timezone-aware UTC now (replaces deprecated datetime.utcnow)."""
    23→    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    24→
    25→
    26→class Candidate(Base):
    27→    """Candidate model - stores candidate profile information."""
    28→
    29→    __tablename__ = 'candidates'
    30→
    31→    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    32→    maimai_id = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False, index=True)
    33→    name = Column(String(100))
    34→    title = Column(String(200))
    35→    company = Column(String(200))
    36→    location = Column(String(100))
    37→    profile_url = Column(Text)
    38→
    39→    # JSON fields for structured data
    40→    basic_info = Column(JSON)
    41→    education = Column(JSON)
    42→    work_experience = Column(JSON)
    43→    skills = Column(JSON)
    44→
    45→    # Metadata
    46→    first_seen = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow)
    47→    last_updated = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, onupdate=_utcnow)
    48→    source_search = Column(Text)  # Search keywords that found this candidate
    49→
    50→    # Relationships
    51→    messages = relationship("Message", back_populates="candidate")
    52→
    53→    def __repr__(self):
    54→        return f"<Candidate(maimai_id='{self.maimai_id}', name='{self.name}', company='{self.company}')>"
    55→
    56→
    57→class Message(Base):
    58→    """Message model - stores sent messages to candidates."""
    59→
    60→    __tablename__ = 'messages'
    61→
    62→    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)

    68→    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow)

    70→    # Relationships

    72→
    73→    def __repr__(self):
    74→        return f"<Message(id={self.id}, candidate_id='{self.candidate_maimai_id}', status='{self.status}')>"
    75→
    76→
